steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to ‘specify when Kenorland formed.’ The notes contain one bullet with that information: ‘Kenorland formed about 2.6 billion years ago.’ The correct answer must include that specific timing, not vague language or unrelated facts.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Long ago, the Earth was home to supercontinents like Kenorland and Rodinia.': ‘Long ago’ is imprecise and does not specify when Kenorland formed. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The supercontinent Kenorland formed about 2.6 billion years ago.': directly states Kenorland’s formation timing using the exact figure from the notes. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Over hundreds of millions of years, the supercontinent cycle results in supercontinents forming and breaking apart.': describes the supercontinent cycle in general — no mention of Kenorland’s specific formation date. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Kenorland was a supercontinent, a single landmass made up of most or all of Earth’s continents.': defines what a supercontinent is but says nothing about when Kenorland formed. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
